name: Nintendo Entertainment System
description: The NES revitalized the home video game industry with accessible hardware and iconic software libraries. It uses the 6502 CPU and has straightforward memory-mapped I/O for games.
funFact: NES cartridges often contained additional chips to expand the system's capabilities.

codingExamples.title: Infinite Loop (6502 Assembly)
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: This code makes the NES do nothing forever by jumping to itself. Useful for basic testing.
explanation: Jumps back to the loop, causing the CPU to loop forever.

codingExamples.title: Hello World Print (Python, Emulation Script)
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: Prints a message using Python, which is used in development scripts and emulators.
explanation: Uses the print function to display text on the screen.

codingExamples.title: Output to Log (JavaScript, NES Tools)
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: Prints a message in JavaScript for tools and NES-related scripts.
explanation: Calls the console.log function with a string.

codingExamples.title: Console Log (TypeScript, NES Tools)
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: This logs a message with TypeScript, useful for NES tool development.
explanation: Prints a message to the console.

codingExamples.title: Print Output (C)
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: Print a message to standard output using C.
explanation: Returns zero to indicate successful completion.

codingExamples.title: Print to Console (C++)
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: Outputs a message to the console using C++.

codingExamples.title: Write Line (C#)
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: Prints a message in C#. Useful for desktop NES utilities.

codingExamples.title: Print Statement (Rust)
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: Prints a message using Rust for NES tool development.
explanation: Prints the string with println macro.

codingExamples.title: Print Message (Go)
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: Print a message with Go, often used for NES ROM tools.

codingExamples.title: Output Message (Java)
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: Print text in Java, typical for NES utility tools.
explanation: Defines the main class and function.
Prints the message to standard output.

codingExamples.title: Infinite Print (BASIC)
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: Keeps printing the message forever. BASIC was common for home computer programming.

codingExamples.title: Lua Print Example
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: Outputs a message with Lua. Lua is used in many emulators.
explanation: Uses print function to display the message.

codingExamples.title: Output String (Ruby)
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: Prints a message with Ruby script, used in automation.
explanation: Uses puts to display the message.

codingExamples.title: Display Message (Swift)
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: Print a message using Swift language, common for macOS tools.
explanation: Calls print function with a string.

codingExamples.title: Print (Kotlin)
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: Shows how to print with Kotlin, now used for scripting and desktop NES tools.

codingExamples.title: Echo Statement (PHP)
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: Echoes a message in PHP, used for web-based NES tools.

codingExamples.title: Bash Echo
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: Prints a message in Bash, useful for automation scripts.
explanation: Runs echo command with the string.

websites.title: NesDev Wiki
websites.description: Technical reference and guides for NES programmers.

name: Game Boy
description: The Game Boy propelled handheld gaming into the mainstream, built on easy-to-learn hardware with a loyal homebrew community.
funFact: Game Boy's screen lag and four-shade grayscale enabled creative graphics techniques.

codingExamples.title: Endless Loop (Z80 Assembly)
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: Causes the Game Boy to do nothing but loop forever. All Game Boy programs start from here.
explanation: Jumps to LOOP again and again.

codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: Outputs a message using Python. Helpful for Game Boy emulation tools.

name: Cisco IOS Router
description: Cisco IOS Routers power data centers and internet infrastructure worldwide. Their configuration is text-based, using a CLI for all interactions.
funFact: The Cisco IOS CLI is used in nearly every big network, from offices to ISPs.

codingExamples.title: Note on Assembly (Not available for CLI users)
codingExamples.code: ; Cisco IOS is not user-programmable in low-level assembly.
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: Typical users cannot write or upload assembly to Cisco routers. All work is done via CLI commands.
explanation: Cisco IOS is managed through CLI, not low-level code.

codingExamples.title: Print Connection Status (Python)
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: Prints a message when an automated script connects to a router.
explanation: Prints a string using Python's print function.

codingExamples.title: Log Connection (JavaScript)
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: Logs to the console in JavaScript, used for automation or configuration tools.
explanation: Uses console.log to display a message.

codingExamples.title: Log Output (TypeScript)
codingExamples.code: console.log('Connecting to Cisco IOS with TypeScript');
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: Same as JavaScript but typed, for network automation scripts.

name: Apple IIe
description: The Apple IIe was a widely used home and classroom computer known for approachability and expandability. It features a 6502 CPU and straightforward hardware.
funFact: Several entire companies were started building add-on cards for the Apple II series.

codingExamples.title: Endless Loop (6502 Assembly)
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: This code causes the Apple IIe to run in a never-ending loop. Many Apple IIe programs start this way.
explanation: Jumps to the loop, repeating forever.

name: Neo Geo MVS
description: The Neo Geo MVS was a revolutionary arcade system, famed for advanced graphics and its massive cartridge games library.
